content.problemStatement:
An array A=[A1,A2,‚Ä¶,AN]A = [A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N]A=[A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč] is said to be good when the following condition holds:


For every pair of subsets XXX and YYY of indices in the range [1,N][1, N][1,N], such that ‚ą£X‚ą£<‚ą£Y‚ą£|X| < |Y|‚ą£X‚ą£<‚ą£Y‚ą£, it holds that the sum of elements in XXX is **strictly smaller** than the sum of elements in YYY.

More formally, it should hold that ‚ąĎi‚ąąXAi<‚ąĎi‚ąąYAi\sum_{i \in X}A_i < \sum_{i \in Y}A_i‚ąĎi‚ąąX‚ÄčAi‚Äč<‚ąĎi‚ąąY‚ÄčAi‚Äč.
For example, [2,2,3][2, 2, 3][2,2,3] is a good array and we can verify this condition. But, [2,2,4][2, 2, 4][2,2,4] is not because consider X={3}X = \{3\}X={3} and Y={1,2}Y = \{1, 2\}Y={1,2}. ‚ąĎi‚ąąXAi=4\sum_{i \in X}A_i = 4‚ąĎi‚ąąX‚ÄčAi‚Äč=4 and ‚ąĎi‚ąąYAi=2+2=4\sum_{i \in Y}A_i = 2 + 2 = 4‚ąĎi‚ąąY‚ÄčAi‚Äč=2+2=4. Both have the same sum 444 despite XXX having fewer elements, and this contradicts the condition.

You are given an array AAA of NNN integers.

Find the number of non-empty subsequences of the array AAA which are good.

A subsequence is an array formed by deleting some (possibly none) elements from the array, but without changing the order of the remaining elements. Note that there are 2N‚ąí12^N - 12N‚ąí1 possible non-empty subsequences of an array with NNN integers.

Note: For a set SSS, ‚ą£S‚ą£|S|‚ą£S‚ą£ denotes the cardinality or size of the set SSS.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.


Output Format
Output the number of good subsequences of AAA.
constraints: **Subtask 4 (13 points):** There are at most 333 distinct values in the array AAA.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
There are 777 subsequences as enlisted - [1],[1],[2],[1,1],[1,2],[1,2][1], [1], [2], [1, 1], [1, 2], [1, 2][1],[1],[2],[1,1],[1,2],[1,2] and [1,1,2][1, 1, 2][1,1,2]. Note that some of them appear multiple times.
All of them except [1,1,2][1, 1, 2][1,1,2] are good, and hence, there are 666 good subsequences.
